Key Finding: Retention Strength Score combines engagement, product depth, relationship strength, card ownership, value, and churn status.
Customers in weaker retention tiers show elevated churn risk and require closer monitoring.
Metric Evidence: Highest churn tier: Critical Retention Risk with 68.98% churn

Key Finding: Strong retention tiers show better relationship strength and lower churn behavior.
Metric Evidence: Best retention tier: Very Strong Retention with 100.0% retention

Key Finding: Risk segments and retention strength tiers together provide better prioritization than single-variable analysis.
Metric Evidence: Customer risk segment + retention strength tier matrix prepared

Key Finding: Sticky customers represent customers with strong behavioral loyalty signals.
Retention scoring should be used as a core dashboard layer for segmentation, filtering, and prioritization.
